next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 15

The Margins smelled like exposure: hot stone, alkali, and old water dragged too far underground. Six weeks earlier Sable would have filed the place under dirt and moved on. Now the heat lay on her scalp, the backs of her hands, and the strip of skin above her collar where the Vault's formal neckline had given way, three weeks earlier, to an unlaced opening she no longer noticed.

She kept track of the two hundred and thirty-eight the way she used to keep track of her schedule: automatically, while she was already doing something else. The schedule had never shifted. The number did, and every drop kept carrying the same contradiction. She had less of what she had been and more of whatever she was becoming.

The settlement appeared where the former supply road bent around a dry wash. Eight structures stood around a collapsed well. Half the roofs had been patched with sheet metal that did not match, and the Hoarded road ended in front of the place as if whoever approved the extension had concluded that eight structures were all the settlement deserved. Sable meant to keep walking. Then she saw the man outside the largest house sorting grain one kernel at a time, and the rhythm of his hands was wrong.

The motion caught at old memory. She had seen it in Council chambers, in the administrative wing, in Lord Aldraste reaching for a glass and arriving there late. The man moved three kernels, paused, moved two more, and then held the third in the air until the instruction reached his fingers.

Sable crossed to the low stone wall opposite him and sat. Dust came through...
open_hooks_to_carry: Second consecutive Kem chapter — POV alternation broken; Vel's inner world goes silent
Communication collapsed to logistics; mechanical cooperation that works despite broken trust
Margins settlement rescue: collapsed mining substrate, family trapped — Vel directs, Kem executes with 6 levels of precise spending (31 to 25)
Dark comedy of the rescue: precision teamwork performed in emotional silence; bystander asks if they're partners, neither answers

Kem's flash of comprehension: exhausted at 25, he briefly understands why someone would choose never to feel any of this — the terror of full embodiment, the Hoarded impulse toward preservation. Not agreement. Comprehension. Then he looks at the rescued family and it passes.
Kem's decision to continue: not survival math, not new information — he started spending for other people and that person doesn't stop at 25
Kem's signet: a Margins child asks about it; Kem explains and is ashamed
Vel consults Maren's route journal; Kem notices the handwriting isn't hers and the care she shows it
prev_tail_excerpt:
...if she needed anything.

He stayed where he was.

The staying did not feel like victory. It felt like setting one hurt down on top of another and calling the stack discipline.

When dawn finally started paling the eastern edge of the fields, it came colorless at first, then yellow and merciless. Dren emerged rubbing his face with one hand and muttering about his arm. Vel came out last, journal packed, expression blank with the kind of tiredness she wore as function. Nobody mentioned the night walk. Nobody mentioned the cough. Nobody mentioned anything from the farmhouse because there was no version of the conversation that would not either reopen it or fake its closure.

They packed in near silence.

Kem slid the signet into the side pocket of his bag instead of his coat. It belonged off his body for now. He checked the wrap on his palm, tightened it, and shouldered the pack. The rough edge of the cloth under the strap pressed into the split skin and reminded him of the grain sacks, the boy's torn collar, the six sacks salvaged instead of nine, the kind of choices that had become normal so gradually he could no longer remember what normal was before them.

They left the weigh station as the sun got its full hand on the land. Dren walked between them again, bad arm close to his ribs. Vel kept the route and Kem kept the silence. The road narrowed, then gave up and became two tracks through broken ground. Somewhere ahead the basin waited. Somewhere behind them the farmer's road had probably finished collapsing. The day already smelled of dust and hot stone.

Kem kept walking.

setups_to_plant.description: Kem's brief understanding of the Hoarded impulse — the terror of embodiment — is a moment of complexity that complicates the novel's thesis without endorsing preservation. Filed for potential Book 2 resonance.

state_deltas_required: The fractured alliance forced into mechanical cooperation; the cost of embodiment at near-zero levels
Kem chooses to continue for non-transactional reasons; mechanical competence demonstrated despite emotional fracture; Kem briefly comprehends the Hoarded impulse toward preservation and rejects it
